message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="team-lead" summary="Task #1 pushed, need 9/9 consensus">
Task #1 (password toggle fix) committed and pushed (fe1009a). CI is running.

ALL REVIEWERS: Please review the changes and send your consent ("I consent") or specific concerns. We need 9/9 consensus before Lea moves to Task #2.

Review the commit: static/js/password-toggle.js and tests/api/register.rs were modified. The fix adds a hidden input that mirrors the password value when the confirm field is removed by the toggle.
</teammate-message>

content.text: Let me review the changes in commit fe1009a.

input.description: Show files changed in commit fe1009a

content.content:
commit fe1009abb1bb596f32bbfcd46fa39e614e33a3e8
Good "git" signature for john@johnwilger.com with ED25519 key SHA256:Fa/i+mjuGYJd8KbzsXhV2I87PNZ8q9S/k8BCW2ayIr0
Author: John Wilger <john@johnwilger.com>
Date:   Sun Feb 8 14:20:38 2026 -0800

    Fix password toggle: add hidden input mirror for password_confirmation
    
    When the password toggle removes the confirm field from the DOM, the form
    no longer sends password_confirmation, causing the server to reject the
    submission. Add a hidden input that mirrors the password value whenever
    the confirm group is removed, and sync it on input events.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 static/js/password-toggle.js | 29 ++++++++++++++++++++++++++---
 tests/api/register.rs        |  4 ++++
 2 files changed, 30 insertions(+), 3 deletions(-)

content.content:
commit fe1009abb1bb596f32bbfcd46fa39e614e33a3e8
Good "git" signature for john@johnwilger.com with ED25519 key SHA256:Fa/i+mjuGYJd8KbzsXhV2I87PNZ8q9S/k8BCW2ayIr0
Author: John Wilger <john@johnwilger.com>
Date:   Sun Feb 8 14:20:38 2026 -0800

    Fix password toggle: add hidden input mirror for password_confirmation
    
    When the password toggle removes the confirm field from the DOM, the form
    no longer sends password_confirmation, causing the server to reject the
    submission. Add a hidden input that mirrors the password value whenever
    the confirm group is removed, and sync it on input events.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

diff --git a/tests/api/register.rs b/tests/api/register.rs
index 0018724..81425f4 100644
--- a/tests/api/register.rs
+++ b/tests/api/register.rs
@@ -334,6 +334,10 @@ async fn password_toggle_script_is_served() {
         body.contains("aria-label"),
         "Script should set aria-label for accessibility"
     );
+    assert!(
+        body.contains("password_confirmation"),
+        "Script should create a hidden input for password_confirmation"
+    );
 }
 
 #[tokio::test]

content.text: Now let me read the full current state of the file to see the complete picture.
Good. I've reviewed the full diff and final state. This is a clean fix. Let me send my consent.

input.content:
**I consent** on Task #1.

Review from the hypermedia/progressive enhancement perspective:

**Progressive enhancement is preserved correctly.** The no-JS baseline is untouched -- the confirm field stays in the DOM, the form POSTs normally with both `password` and `password_confirmation`. The hidden input is only created by JavaScript, which is exactly right. The server contract (`password_confirmation` as a required field) is maintained in both paths.

**Locality of behavior is good.** The hidden input is scoped to the password group, created and destroyed alongside the toggle state. The `input` event listener syncs the mirror value, so the hidden field is always current when the form submits. No server-side changes needed -- the form POST sends the same fields either way.

**One observation (non-blocking):** The register form doesn't currently use HTMX (no `hx-post` or similar attributes), so there's no interaction between this fix and HTMX. That's correct for now -- registration is a full page POST/redirect, which is the right pattern for a one-time account creation flow. If we ever HTMX-enhance the registration form, the hidden input approach will still work fine since it's part of the DOM when the form serializes.

Clean fix, minimal change, no over-engineering. Approved.
input.summary: Consent on Task #1 - progressive enhancement preserved

routing.summary: Consent on Task #1 - progressive enhancement preserved
routing.content:
**I consent** on Task #1.

Review from the hypermedia/progressive enhancement perspective:

**Progressive enhancement is preserved correctly.** The no-JS baseline is untouched -- the confirm field stays in the DOM, the form POSTs normally with both `password` and `password_confirmation`. The hidden input is only created by JavaScript, which is exactly right. The server contract (`password_confirmation` as a required field) is maintained in both paths.

**Locality of behavior is good.** The hidden input is scoped to the password group, created and destroyed alongside the toggle state. The `input` event listener syncs the mirror value, so the hidden field is always current when the form submits. No server-side changes needed -- the form POST sends the same fields either way.

**One observation (non-blocking):** The register form doesn't currently use HTMX (no `hx-post` or similar attributes), so there's no interaction between this fix and HTMX. That's correct for now -- registration is a full page POST/redirect, which is the right pattern for a one-time account creation flow. If we ever HTMX-enhance the registration form, the hidden input approach will still work fine since it's part of the DOM when the form serializes.

Clean fix, minimal change, no over-engineering. Approved.

content.text: Consent sent for Task #1. Standing by for Task #2 review when Lea begins the CSS gaps work.
